title: Shop Hourly Rate Calculator
description: Calculates the true hourly cost of operating a manufacturing shop, incorporating direct labor, overhead, machine costs, and efficiency losses per industrial engineering standards (ISO 9000, Lean, Six Sigma, WERC).

inputs.label: Direct Labor Hourly Rate
inputs.businessContext: Average hourly wage paid to direct production workers, including base pay and shift differentials.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average hourly wage paid to direct production workers, including base pay and shift differentials.

inputs.label: Number of Direct Labor Employees
inputs.businessContext: Total headcount of workers directly involved in production.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total headcount of workers directly involved in production.

inputs.label: Annual Operating Hours per Employee
inputs.businessContext: Standard annual hours per employee (e.g., 52 weeks * 40 hours). Adjust for overtime or part-time.

businessContext_i18n.en: Standard annual hours per employee (e.g., 52 weeks * 40 hours). Adjust for overtime or part-time.

inputs.label: Total Annual Overhead Costs
inputs.businessContext: All indirect costs: rent, utilities, management salaries, insurance, taxes, maintenance, IT, etc.

businessContext_i18n.en: All indirect costs: rent, utilities, management salaries, insurance, taxes, maintenance, IT, etc.

inputs.label: Total Annual Machine Costs
inputs.businessContext: Depreciation, lease payments, repairs, tooling, and energy costs for all production equipment.

businessContext_i18n.en: Depreciation, lease payments, repairs, tooling, and energy costs for all production equipment.

inputs.label: Average Shop Efficiency (%)
inputs.businessContext: Overall equipment effectiveness (OEE) or labor efficiency, accounting for downtime, setup, and speed loss.

businessContext_i18n.en: Overall equipment effectiveness (OEE) or labor efficiency, accounting for downtime, setup, and speed loss.

inputs.label: Quality Yield (%)
inputs.businessContext: First-pass yield or overall yield; rework and scrap increase effective hourly cost.

businessContext_i18n.en: First-pass yield or overall yield; rework and scrap increase effective hourly cost.

inputs.label: Shift Premium Factor
inputs.businessContext: 1.0 for single shift, 1.1 for two-shift, 1.2 for three-shift operations (accounts for shift differentials).

businessContext_i18n.en: 1.0 for single shift, 1.1 for two-shift, 1.2 for three-shift operations (accounts for shift differentials).

inputs.label: Include Benefits & Payroll Taxes
inputs.businessContext: If true, adds 30% burden to direct labor rate for benefits, FICA, workers' comp, etc.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, adds 30% burden to direct labor rate for benefits, FICA, workers' comp, etc.

inputs.label: Overhead Allocation Method
inputs.businessContext: Basis for distributing overhead to hourly rate. Direct labor hours is most common in job shops.

businessContext_i18n.en: Basis for distributing overhead to hourly rate. Direct labor hours is most common in job shops.

rules.message: Efficiency below 50% indicates severe operational issues; consider Lean assessment.
Yield below 80% suggests significant quality problems; Six Sigma project recommended.
Overhead exceeds 50% of direct labor cost; review overhead structure.

thresholds.message: Shop hourly rate exceeds $150/hr; benchmark against industry.
Efficiency critically low; immediate process improvement needed.

formulas.description: Adjusted direct labor rate including benefits and shift premium
Total annual direct labor hours available
Overhead cost allocated per direct labor hour
Machine cost allocated per direct labor hour
Sum of labor, overhead, and machine costs before efficiency and quality adjustments
Combined factor for efficiency and quality losses (Lean/Six Sigma approach)
Primary result: fully burdened hourly rate accounting for all costs and losses

primary.label: True Shop Hourly Rate
primary.description: The actual cost per productive hour, including all direct and indirect costs, adjusted for efficiency and quality losses.

effective_labor_rate.label: Effective Labor Rate
effective_labor_rate.description: Direct labor rate after benefits and shift premium.

overhead_rate_per_hour.label: Overhead Rate per Hour
overhead_rate_per_hour.description: Overhead allocated per direct labor hour.

machine_rate_per_hour.label: Machine Rate per Hour
machine_rate_per_hour.description: Machine cost allocated per direct labor hour.

gross_hourly_rate.label: Gross Hourly Rate
gross_hourly_rate.description: Sum of labor, overhead, and machine rates before efficiency adjustment.

efficiency_adjustment_factor.label: Efficiency Adjustment Factor
efficiency_adjustment_factor.description: Product of efficiency and yield percentages.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Downtime Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Hours lost due to unplanned downtime, setup, and maintenance.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Speed Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Running equipment below rated speed (e.g., due to aging or suboptimal settings).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Quality Loss (Rework & Scrap)
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Time and material wasted on defective products.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Indirect Labor Burden
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Time spent by direct labor on non-productive activities (cleaning, meetings, training).

suggestedActions.label: Implement TPM (Total Productive Maintenance)
suggestedActions.description: Reduce unplanned downtime by 50% through preventive maintenance and operator training.

suggestedActions.label: Deploy Six Sigma DMAIC for top defect causes
suggestedActions.description: Target the top 3 defect types to improve first-pass yield.

suggestedActions.label: Review overhead allocation and reduce non-value-added costs
suggestedActions.description: Lean office and indirect cost reduction.

suggestedActions.label: Standardize work procedures to reduce speed loss
suggestedActions.description: Create and train on best-practice standard work.
